subject: Move Storage access to RWO
commit_message:
Move Storage access to RWO

Today when deploying Robot with OOM, the PersistentVolumeClaim needs the
"ReadWriteMany" (or "RWX") capability.

According to Kubernetes Documentation (https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes/#access-modes),
ReadWriteMany stands for "the volume can be mounted as read-write by many nodes".

That means that a particular PVC needs to be read and written from many pods.
That also means that your code takes that into account and do the work to avoid
write at the same place at the same time.

An issue on RWX mode is that most "official" storage driver from Kubernetes doesn't
support it (13 over the 19 drivers doesn't support it, espacially OpenStack, Amazon
and Google storage classes).

Robot PVC is used only for one Robot instance. Thus we don't need RWX.

Issue-ID: INT-1230
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: I300e34a9d4be40b36153812d2a99c458cae6b2c9

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

i am working into splitting out the robot results volume so that it is written to by one volume and displayed by a seperate lighthttpd volume. would this impact that?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> i am working into splitting out the robot results volume so that it is written to by one volume and displayed by a seperate lighthttpd volume. would this impact that?

If one (robot) write and many (Lighthttpd, other) reads, there's no impact IMHO

Patch Set 1:

per https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/26567 i think it certainly may work with our nfs setup, but i dont think that it is guaranteed to be that way depending on driver - i am not sure its safe to rely on undefined behavior :)

Patch Set 1:

Yes Daniel you're right.
Where I'm a bit worried, is that RWX needs may be blocking for deploying into a public KaaS (and especially Vexxhost which is the "LF" one) as RWX is not really supported by OpenStack Cinder Storage Driver.
But I do understand your need (one POD = one function) so I've no good proposition...
I'll think and search to see if we can find another way :)

Patch Set 1:

we could get crafty and have a shared non permanent storage and then make a 3rd pod that reads the permananet storage and shares it in the nonpermanent storage?
